decision-tree.root-question: Does the workflow contain any irreversible action (DB writes, payments, deploys, external emails)?

branch.conclusion: Primary pattern stays the one from the rest of the tree; ADD `hitl_gates` listing every irreversible action. HITL is overlay, not replacement.

branch.question: Is the task choice-of-specialist with a known classifier (accuracy ≥0.85)?

branch.conclusion: Pick `router`. Document classifier_accuracy; fall back to a generalist agent below threshold.

branch.question: Do agents need shared mutable state visible to all (workspace, blackboard)?

branch.conclusion: Pick `blackboard`. Document concurrency_plan (single-writer queue / CRDT / DB transactions).

branch.question: Is iterative refinement with adversarial critique needed?

branch.conclusion: Pick `generator_critic`. Generator + critic on different model families; downstream impl = `multi-agent-conversational`.

branch.question: Is the task open-ended with a halting criterion (refine until quality / convergence)?

branch.conclusion: Pick `loop`. Halting predicate required; downstream impl = `multi-agent-conversational` with `max_turns` cap.

branch.question: Is the task decomposable into independent parallel subtasks?

branch.conclusion: Pick `parallel_fanout`. Strong synthesizer at gather step (opus-grade); downstream impl = `multi-agent-basics`.
Pick `hierarchical`. Manager + stateless workers + synthesis; downstream impl = `multi-agent-hierarchical`.

example.p: 1500-word explainer requiring factual accuracy; no irreversible action; one writer + one critic; no shared state; needs critique → q-irreversible=no → q-classifier=no → q-shared-state=no → q-quality-loop=yes → conclusion `generator_critic` (downstream `multi-agent-conversational`).
